title: Jeju Yacht Tour Honest Review: Grande Bleu Catamaran
content:
Published: November 2024 · Last updated: November 2024 By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, running 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port in Seogwipo.

If you're on your second or third trip to Jeju and tired of the same volcano hikes and waterfall queues, a catamaran sail is the quiet alternative most guidebooks skip. Here's an honest look at what the ride is actually like, what's included, and whether it's worth the won.

Quick Facts

  • Location: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju Island, South Korea (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Capacity: 91 guests across two catamarans (44 + 47)
  • Price: Adult ₩60,000–70,000 / Child ₩40,000
  • Includes: Unlimited draft beer, wine, tangerine juice, snacks, ramyeon, fishing
  • Operating since: 2011 · 100,000+ guests served
  • Book: Klook (English booking, instant confirmation)

What Makes Grande Bleu Different

Most Jeju "yacht tours" are single-hull motorboats that bounce around the moment the wind picks up. Grande Bleu runs twin-hull catamarans — the same boat design used by Caribbean charter fleets — which absorbs roughly 90% of wave force. At an average 37cm swell, you can stand at the bar holding a glass of wine without spilling it.

That's the practical difference. It's also the reason it's the only catamaran brand certified for commercial sail in Korea, and why Shilla Hotel and Parnas Jeju route their guests here.

For a 40-something traveller who's done Bali speedboats and Greek island ferries, the comparison that lands closest is a small Mediterranean day-sail — minus the cattle-class crowds.

FAQ

What is the cheapest Jeju yacht tour? Grande Bleu's Luxury Daytime Tour starts at ₩60,000 per adult and ₩40,000 per child, with unlimited food and drinks included. It's the lowest entry point for a certified catamaran sail in Jeju.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? The standard sail is 60 minutes from Daepo Port in Seogwipo, covering six coastal landmarks including Elephant Rock and the Wolpyeong basalt columns. Boarding starts roughly 20 minutes before departure.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes. Draft beer, red and white wine, Jeju tangerine juice, bottled water, local snacks, and freshly cooked Korean ramyeon are all unlimited and free onboard, with no extra charges or upsells.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? Grande Bleu sails from Daepo Port in Seogwipo on Jeju Island's south coast (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E). Free parking is available; public buses run from central Seogwipo with a short walk to the dock.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran design absorbs around 90% of wave motion, making it noticeably more stable than single-hull boats. A licensed captain and engineer are onboard, and the operator has served 100,000+ guests since 2011.

What's the best time for sunset cruise in Jeju? October and November offer the most reliable conditions — calm seas, mild 18–22°C temperatures, and long golden-hour light. Book the late-afternoon slot to catch the 360-degree sunset window.
